title: Chapter 147 – Black Robe (5)
content:
Everything proceeded smoothly.

Massive tents were erected in a circle around the main fortress's central training ground.

It wasn't merely a training ground — it served as the venue for various ceremonies, including promotions.

The layout allowed hundreds to sit facing the center.

"More to the left!"

"Lift on two!"

"We're short a stone pillar. Bring a medium one!"

Dozens of servants, unnamed disciples, and white-robed warriors hauled stone materials and hoisted enormous carpets into place.

At Desolate Fortress, where wood was seldom used out of respect for the noble Fortress Lord, they made do with cloth and stone.

Though constructing seating from cloth and stone alone was no simple task, the unnamed disciples and white-robed warriors channeled powerful internal energy as they raised stone pillars throughout the grounds.

The teamwork was extraordinary — faster than any labor force a government office could mobilize.

"Never thought menial work could resemble military operations. Couldn't have imagined this when I was just a troublemaker at home. Fascinating. Profoundly uninteresting."

That was Lazy Flame Dragon, rambling as he pleased. Several of Radiant Demon Wing's people had also been conscripted as laborers.

Hyeon Won-chang glanced at him.

He was carrying a stone pillar as large as himself.

Despite his complaints, he was doing the work of more than three typical white-robed warriors on his own.

He strolled along with the casual swagger of a spoiled young master, yet his balance was flawless.

"What an unusual way to train footwork."

Hyeon Won-chang, who had been studying Lazy Flame Dragon, pursed his lips slightly.

The hero's headband tied around the Desolate Hero's forehead was caked with dust — the result of long labor.

"It's quite remarkable that you noticed, Senior Hyeon."

Shin So-bin smiled from beside him, carrying an armful of stone slabs with surprising strength.

Like most gifted young martial artists who took care with their appearance, she wore her hair in a neat braid that fell past her waist.

Unlike Hyeon Won-chang, she looked immaculate. Her Body Protection Technique was that skillful.

The three had been drafted as the junior members of Radiant Demon Wing.

Their experience and rank made them suitable for menial tasks — hardly a position from which to escape.

"You could carry more, but you don't. Strange behavior for the number one young talent of Jinan."

At Hyeon Won-chang's complaint, the corners of Lazy Flame Dragon's mouth curled upward.

"A person need only do as much work as their rank demands. For the head of a martial school, both martial skill and reputation are measured, but from a newly entered disciple, no one expects even the correct stance of the basic forms. If a nameless disciple does this much, shouldn't you be grateful? Isn't it rather shameful for a white-robed warrior to struggle with his strength?"

"Are you committing insubordination?"

"Don't take my candid words so harshly. Sects can rot like stagnant water if they aren't careful. That's how my family fell. I said that mobilizing commoners for labor was madness, but I was treated like a stray dog on the roadside. Dismissed without a thought."

"Candid words?"

"Isn't that putting it gently? Seems like you can't even keep pace with the temporary commander's footwork."

"So you're provoking a white-robed warrior as a nameless disciple?"

Hyeon Won-chang answered Lazy Flame Dragon's sly words without hesitation.

Did he have no objection to that characterization? Lazy Flame Dragon merely raised his eyes and let his mouth fall slightly open. Ah, so that's how it is.

*THUD!*

While driving the stone pillar into the training ground floor, he spoke again.

"This is what happens when a sect stubbornly insists on using only stone. Originally, a martial artist's duty is to cultivate martial arts and character, to pursue the ultimate and spread renown through chivalry — yet here, no one complains about being drafted for manual labor. Truly strange."

He spoke like the stiff young master of a noble household. His unhurried manner of speech suited the role oddly well.

Shin So-bin shook her head.

"This isn't just any event — it's a black-rank promotion ceremony. Regardless of the outcome, the ceremony itself is remarkable."

"I roughly understand. That's the fortress's stance, you mean."

"Of course it is. Think of it like selecting the captain of Shaolin's Eighteen Arhats or the head of Mount Hua's Plum Blossom Swordsmen. No — the main fortress's leader promotion test carries even greater weight."

"Hmm."

"What single sect is larger than ours? This has to be one of the grandest ceremonies in the fortress. A rare festival. The major sects outside must be watching in all sorts of ways."

"That must be true. But it feels strange that people who'd be treated as masters outside are silently hauling loads like laborers. It makes it difficult even for me to slack off. Oh? That fellow over there — his balance looks precarious... no, he has decent footwork after all."

"When the Fortress Lord herself will attend, what wouldn't we do? There's no master in the fortress who doesn't revere her."

"Well... she was overwhelming in every way. Though I only saw her once."

By now, Lazy Flame Dragon had mastered the art of speaking informally by disguising it as muttering to himself.

It was probably his greatest area of growth since entering Desolate Fortress.

He glanced up at the high seat. The white stone chair where the Fortress Lord would sit.

Above tiers of marble stacked like an altar.

It had been there from the beginning, overlooking the training ground.

A setup resembling a great faction's banquet hall was taking shape around the Fortress Lord's throne.

Black-rank leader promotion test. The spectacle heralded a grand ceremony.

"Back to work."

Even Shin So-bin said nothing more. The Hwangbo young master was devilishly skilled at reading the room.

Even as the precious jade of the Shin family, there wasn't much to criticize. She seemed to have given up trying.

Lazy Flame Dragon ignored her words entirely. Instead, he asked abruptly:

"Has Desolate Fortress's Law Enforcement Leader ever lost in such a test? Since she rarely appears outside the fortress, even my family only knew she was powerful."

Rumors had already spread.

Who would face Lightning Genius in the promotion duel had become the hottest topic of discussion.

Interest reportedly surged the moment the Law Enforcement Leader's name was mentioned. They called it the prospect of a rare duel.

"Once."

As even Hyeon Won-chang drew closer, Shin So-bin spoke.

"The current Divine Sword Group Leader won. Otherwise, she's undefeated. After losing to the Law Enforcement Leader, Radiant Demon Wing Leader and Azure Sky Leader faced each other. It took considerable time before they could challenge again."

[TL Note- it seems to be referring to the Law Enforcement Leader fought with the current Divine Sword Group Leader for the position.]

"She must be extraordinarily powerful. I've seen Azure Sky Leader — a supreme sword master."

Lazy Flame Dragon lightly stroked his chin.

"That's to be expected. No leader is anything less than a supreme master. Though there are supreme masters even within the black-rank realm, it's not a realm we can presume to discuss. In any case, rather than all that..."

"There's more?"

"The nature of the Law Enforcement Division itself is the real issue. She knows every martial art practiced by Desolate Fortress masters in positions of importance. That's what rumors say. She needs that knowledge to efficiently apprehend warriors who break the law."

"Like my family's law enforcement department. Terrifying."

"The Law Enforcement Leader has everything. Beauty, character, martial arts, technique analysis. She's the idol of female warriors walking the martial path in the fortress. Senior Mi-ryeo would be no different."

Shin So-bin's focus drifted for a moment. She looked almost dazed.

Watching her, Lazy Flame Dragon shook his head slightly. There are no normal ones among prestigious family children.

"So you're saying Lightning Genius's odds aren't good?"

"That's hard to conclude. For reasons only I know."

Her gaze sharpened immediately. Hyeon Won-chang asked with interest.

"Is there some secret between you and Young Master Jeong?"

"Senior Lightning Genius told me something once."

Shin So-bin murmured.

"The reason he revealed his martial arts wasn't solely for merit. He actually hopes for counter techniques to emerge."

"Right, I know that story too."

Hyeon Won-chang turned away without lingering.

His departing steps seemed noticeably light, befitting one who claimed to be Lightning Genius's close friend.

Only Lazy Flame Dragon remained to hear her out.

"He hopes for it? What's the reason?"

"I won't tell you."

Shin So-bin said while tilting her head with exaggerated innocence. The corners of her mouth twisted upward ever so slightly.

Lazy Flame Dragon let out a hollow laugh. As if dealing with a strange one playing the role of mischievous superior.

*THUD! BOOM!*

Time passed in idle conversation. The promotion ceremony venue was gradually taking shape.

There was good reason for the trouble of decorating the central training ground instead of using one of the existing platforms within the vast fortress.

The symbolism of black-rank masters was that great.

A sword shouldering one axis of missions that spanned the vast Central Plains.

It was a position that demanded the ability to fully command one elite group of a major faction.

***

The Administration Office was pushing forward at an extraordinary pace. They had nearly lost two purple-rank supreme masters.

Since a grand ceremony served as an excellent means of drawing warriors' attention and raising morale, they were staging a promotion ceremony of unprecedented scale.

Below the dueling platform.

Hundreds had already taken their seats. The atmosphere hummed with excitement, curiosity, and nervous anticipation.

Even just the massive circular tents evoked a grand royal court ceremony.

Warriors whose honed spirits cut like blades, servants who had groomed themselves meticulously, craftsmen wearing their rough appearances with pride, and various guests invited to observe.

The noise was considerable.

*HAH! HAAH!*

At the center — the dueling platform.

A sword dance was being performed to stoke the atmosphere before the ceremony.

Over a dozen swords carved elegant arcs in perfect unison.

In one corner, resident artists played zithers and sang.

Beautiful melodies flowed, their verses extolling the warrior spirit. The music harmonized with the sword dance.

But the masters were busy talking.

"Speaking of Lightning Genius's defining quality — it's flow. His techniques aren't drawn out. But they're powerful."

"That matches what I've seen. It was mainly short strikes. The timing of his martial arts seemed to depend on instinct."

"He doesn't seem to emphasize a continuous flow. Instead, he appears to gain power from the brevity."

"It's a martial art specialized for quick engagements. Every movement is urgent. It's as though he's being chased by something..."

"That's right. They were all single strikes. Devastating in the moment, but..."

"The weakness is clear. When facing a master, remember two things: don't get drawn into prolonged exchanges, and don't give him time to adapt to your martial arts."

"His style is easy to disrupt. He has to overcome that with quick thinking. It seems he's managed it well so far."

"That's why he's at the black-rank promotion ceremony. It means he's seized the critical moments in life-and-death battles. That's impossible without a natural instinct for pivotal openings."

Sharp insights flashed through the air.

Not just blue-ranks but the handful of black-rank supreme masters dissected his martial arts with thoroughness.

Most fortress masters devoted their lives to protecting commoners and refining their arts. An event as compelling as today's was exceedingly rare.

How advanced were Lightning Genius's martial arts? Could he withstand the Law Enforcement Leader's techniques, reinforced by counter-analysis?

Would a new Radiant Demon Wing Leader be born here today?

The question consumed everyone. Not a single person present could feign indifference.

Even the elder council's venerable masters, who had passed on the blue-rank promotion ceremony, attended in force.

"She's early."

Radiant Demon Wing Leader, seated below the wide, elevated platform, murmured suddenly. He was looking at the sky.

A slender figure was blocking the sun. An elegant shadow with arms outstretched, gradually growing larger as it descended.

The Body Protection Technique carrying her through the air looked inhuman. It called to mind an immortal's art.

*FLUTTER!*

With the sound of a pitch-black long robe billowing, she alighted at the center of the dueling platform.

The artists performing sword dance and playing instruments stopped at once. The beautiful, noble supreme master touched down lightly on the marble.

*STEP*

Her long pitch-black robe trailed along the ground like the hem of a palace gown. The Law Enforcement Leader had appeared first.

The black-rank leaders' martial attire varied according to personal preference, issued by the Administration Office accordingly.

Rumors held that the Law Enforcement Leader, who deeply revered the Fortress Lord, had requested a robe just one inch shorter than her Lord's.

It was the longest robe among all seventeen leaders.

"Oh."

Hyeon Won-chang's exclamation rang out briefly. Seated in a corner due to his low rank, he quickly covered his mouth.

This was the arrival of a master said to have fully systematized her clan's abilities into martial arts. A sudden hush descended.

No fortress warrior wanted to give the Law Enforcement Leader any reason for displeasure. Even the elder council felt the same.

"..."

The silence lingered. Even the white sunlight beating down seemed to freeze.

Then —

*STEP*

A boy wearing a blue long robe slowly ascended.

A path opened through the gaps left by the artists already streaming off the platform.

Jeong Yeon-shin adjusted the hem of his blue sleeve and stepped onto the dueling platform.

His appearance, wearing two swords, was no different from usual.

"Hah."

This time it was Shin So-bin.

Jeong Yeon-shin had purified himself through bathing, cleansing both body and energy channels. His appearance was luminous.

On the dueling platform, the figure of a young swordsman straight from legend was revealed. The blood of parents said to have been extraordinarily beautiful was coming into full bloom.

His slightly melancholic eyes, set beneath thick brows alongside a sharp nose, only deepened his striking appearance.

Standing before the Law Enforcement Leader with her rare, noble beauty, he looked magnificent.

After regarding her briefly, Jeong Yeon-shin turned his head.

"No need for courtesy."

A clear voice reached him before the boy could speak.

The sun, still climbing, had just caught the edge of the inner fortress roof at one side of the training ground.

The stone chair placed beneath a thick cypress tree.

As brilliant white sunlight blazed diagonally through the shade, it rippled with the subtle color of grass leaves where it touched the Fortress Lord's hair.

The peerless beauty who had been reclining with legs elegantly crossed opened her eyes. Beneath long, sweeping lashes, dark green pupils slowly appeared.

"Think of etiquette when you don the black robe. You may begin."

Her gaze, looking intently downward, was fathomless. The Fortress Lord's pupils were unchanging — like an abyss.

They seemed to envelop the boy's upward gaze.

*SWOOSH*

Meanwhile, the Law Enforcement Leader had already knelt on one knee, then risen.

Unlike other nobles, her eyes were black. She turned her head, and those cold dark irises fixed on Jeong Yeon-shin.

It didn't appear to stem from emotion. Rather, she showed the same consistent indifference she had displayed toward the boy from the start.

'She is a noble swordsman.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. He felt it the instant their eyes met.

The virtues inherent to a true swordsman were fully present. Tranquility like a clear mirror.

"..."

Without preamble, the two masters faced each other. They entered the contest immediately.

Both were of a temperament that required no lengthy explanations.

The silence held.

Only the air between them danced ceaselessly. The dry wind gradually accumulated force waves.

The ground began to be sharply scored. It was a battle at the level where shock waves could be produced through internal energy alone, without drawing swords.

Supreme masters competing in the black-rank realm.

*BOOM!*

Suddenly, true steps thundered out — from beneath Jeong Yeon-shin's feet as they rolled with power.

From his waist, he drew the Desolate sword first.

The instant the white blade rose, warping the sunlight, the Law Enforcement Leader was already striking with hers.

*CLANG!*

In that instant, Jeong Yeon-shin's Desolate sword shattered upward. The gap in their realms was absolute.

The sword that had been with him since his entry into the fortress flashed through his vision as it broke apart in a single exchange.

The boy was not flustered.

He reached out toward the fragments of the iron that had been like a companion.

Following his palm's motion, petals of light distorted.

Scattering sunlight in rivulets, divine technique verses bloomed from the very first exchange.

*RING!*

It was instantaneous. Myriad Heavens Flower Rain enveloped the Law Enforcement Leader's entire body.

Many of the watching masters sprang to their feet.
